market_context: Risk-off tape: CPI back above 4% (Benzinga 06-10 'Inflation Is Back Above 4%'), China-Taiwan tensions pressuring semis, and the Iran truce 'looks more fragile' (Benzinga 06-10 market wrap). VOO fell -3.8% over 5 sessions (696.12 to 667.09). No current holdings, so no holdings-specific web search was required this run. JNJ is the only name with fresh, non-chatter catalysts today (IMAAVY wAIHA data, presbyopia IOL rollout expansion) and is re-signaled as a buy on its own merits; the engine is expected to net this against the buy already pending from the overnight run. UNH headlines were exclusively analyst price-target raises and CNBC chatter, which this desk ignores per policy. All other names: macro-driven declines with no company-specific catalyst, so they default to hold.

signals.thesis: Catalyst stream continues: 06-11 IMAAVY showed rapid, durable anemia improvement in wAIHA (no FDA-approved therapies) and J&J expanded US rollout of presbyopia-correcting IOLs; follows 06-09 MHRA nipocalimab authorization and 06-08 $1B Firefly Bio deal. Price 238.52 is a 30-day high, +6.7% over 5 sessions, against a weak tape. Falsified if price closes below 230 or talc litigation escalates beyond the 06-10 $32M LA mesothelioma verdict.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-11: J&J Says IMAAVY Showed Rapid And Durable Anemia Improvement In wAIHA
Benzinga 2026-06-11: J&J Expands US Roll-Out Of Presbyopia-Correcting Intraocular Lenses
Benzinga 2026-06-10: J&J Fined $32M By LA Jury Over Mesothelioma Death (risk factor)

signals.thesis: Down -6.0% over 5 sessions (311.21 to 291.48) on macro, not company news: today's headlines are industry-comparison and Cramer chatter; the one notable item, 'Apple's AI Reveal Hands An Unexpected Win To Google, NVIDIA' (06-10), is mildly negative for Apple's AI positioning. No catalyst to buy the dip, no thesis-breaking negative to sell into.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-10: Apple's AI Reveal Hands An Unexpected Win To Google, NVIDIA

signals.thesis: Sharpest decliner on the list: -7.1% over 5 sessions and -13.7% from the 06-01 high (460.48 to 397.41), with a concrete negative catalyst — Bloomberg-reported 'significant' Xbox layoffs under the new CEO (06-10) — plus a Verge-reported internal restriction of a vendor AI tool (06-10). Falling-knife conditions with no support confirmed; no position to sell. Hold, do not buy the dip.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-10: 'Xbox Plans Significant Layoffs as It Transforms Under New CEO' - Bloomberg
Benzinga 2026-06-10: 'Microsoft Restricts Claude Fable For Employees Over Data Retention Concerns' - The Verge

signals.thesis: Genuine positive catalysts today — WSJ-exclusive AI healthcare model with Abridge, anchor investment in KKR's Helix Digital Infrastructure (both 06-11) — but the stock is -6.7% over 5 sessions with China-Taiwan tensions explicitly pressuring semis (06-10 market wrap). Catalysts are partnership-stage, not revenue events; insufficient to override the sector-level geopolitical risk. The 'analyst calls NVDA undervalued' headline is price-target chatter, ignored.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-11: 'Nvidia Is Developing an AI Healthcare Model With Startup Abridge' - WSJ
Benzinga 2026-06-10: CPI Saves Market; China-Taiwan Tensions Hurt Semis

signals.thesis: Only company-specific item is negative: a California judge denied Google/Meta motions for a new trial in the youth social-media-addiction case (Reuters via Benzinga, 06-10), an ongoing legal overhang. Rest of the headline list is AI-industry noise not specific to Alphabet. Price -0.8% over 5 sessions, holding up better than the tape; no catalyst either direction.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-10: 'California Judge Denies Google And Meta Motions Seeking New Trial' - Reuters

signals.thesis: Strong momentum (+8.1% over 5 sessions, +9.9% over 30 days) but every headline in today's snapshot is analyst price-target action (Mizuho to $460, Morgan Stanley to $453, KeyBanc upgrade) or CNBC 'final trades' chatter — all ignored per desk policy. No fundamental catalyst this desk can act on; chasing momentum on PT raises alone is not a thesis.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-08: Mizuho Maintains Outperform on UnitedHealth, Raises PT to $460 (chatter, cited as evidence of absence of real catalysts)
